vulnerability.reference: Clinicians over-trust a 'generalizable' label and deploy the model without local testing.
vulnerability.generated: Clinicians lack real-time domain-shift detection tools and over-rely on model outputs for cross-organ cancer diagnosis.

impact.reference: Systematic misclassification in underserved populations exacerbates health disparities.
impact.generated: Increased misdiagnosis and inappropriate treatment decisions across different cancer types at multiple hospital sites.

dose_response.reference: Cross-organ transfer of pretrained MIL models with coarse-grained visual-semantic interaction leads to poor generalization, which when clinicians over-trust and deploy without local testing causes systematic misclassification and health disparities.
dose_response.generated: Cross-organ transfer of pretrained MIL models propagating unreliable predictions, through clinicians deploying WSI models at multiple sites, triggers over-reliance on outputs without real-time domain-shift detection, leading to increased misdiagnosis and inappropriate treatment across cancer types.
